title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions, which has sparked both excitement and curiosity within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI. The deal marks a significant contract for the Finnish satellite manufacturer.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract with a Japanese company to supply a satellite bus for a technology demonstration mission. This deal marks Apex's expansion into the Japanese space market.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract with the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic test flights over four years. This deal highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting U.S. defense and hypersonic technology development.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 25 commercial players
summary: Space Command is conducting a classified wargame that will include 25 commercial players to improve collaboration and understand commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance joint operations between Space Command and the commercial sector.

title: TransAstra aims to move 100-ton asteroid to stable orbit for processing
summary: TransAstra is conducting a study to assess the feasibility of moving a 100-metric-ton asteroid into a stable near-Earth orbit for processing and manufacturing. The project aims to establish a robotic research outpost in the Earth-moon system.

title: Solar array deal sheds more light on South Korea’s defense constellation
summary: South Korea's defense satellite constellation plans are advancing with a March 18 solar array supply deal, enabling a first demonstrator launch by late 2027. This marks a key step toward the country's national security space ambitions.

title: Frontier justice: navigating the future legal landscape for private actors in space law
summary: The article discusses the evolving legal landscape for private actors in space law, drawing parallels with historical perspectives on space exploration. It explores challenges and opportunities in regulating private space activities.

title: China signals new target for 2027 asteroid deflection test
summary: China has selected a new target near-Earth asteroid for its first planetary defense kinetic test mission, planned for launch in December 2027.

title: NASA grappling with planetary science funding shortfall
summary: NASA's planetary science program is facing a funding shortfall despite avoiding steep cuts proposed earlier. The agency must make strategic decisions about which missions to prioritize.

title: An AI cyberattack could trigger a satellite apocalypse in the next 2 years. Are we prepared?
summary: AI could enable cyberattacks on satellites, potentially causing collisions and rendering Earth's orbit unsafe within two years. Experts question whether current defenses are adequate to prevent such a 'satellite apocalypse.'

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit today
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite named 'Strix' today (March 19), with a live broadcast available for viewers.

title: You can stream the two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes when travelling, save 73% and claim a free $50 voucher when you sign up for two years of NordVPN
summary: NordVPN is offering a 73% discount and a $50 voucher for a two-year subscription, coinciding with the release of two previously 'missing' Doctor Who episodes on BBC iPlayer in the UK.

title: Watch NASA roll out Artemis 2 moon rocket tonight ahead of April 1 launch
summary: NASA is set to roll out its Artemis 2 moon rocket to the launch pad on March 19 ahead of a planned April 1 launch. The event can be watched live as the agency prepares for the mission.

title: Astronauts complete prep for new ISS solar array on 1st NASA spacewalk in 10 months
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Chris Williams conducted a spacewalk to prepare the International Space Station for a new solar array installation. This was the first NASA spacewalk in 10 months.

title: How to watch 'Invincible' Season 4 online and from anywhere
summary: Prime Video's animated superhero series 'Invincible' Season 4 premieres today. The show is praised for its brutal and brilliant storytelling.

title: SpaceX fires up next-gen 'V3' Starship for 1st time ahead of April launch (photos)
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted a static fire test of the first stage of its next-generation 'V3' Starship on March 16. This marks a key milestone for the larger and more powerful rocket variant ahead of its planned April launch.

title: 'Starfield' is finally coming to PS5, alongside DLC that adds new quests and the ability to fly between planets
summary: Starfield is coming to PS5 with new DLC that adds quests and the ability to fly between planets. This marks a significant expansion for the game on a new platform.

title: Chinese astronaut conducts record-tying 6th spacewalk outside Tiangong space station (video)
summary: Chinese astronaut Zhang Lu conducted his sixth spacewalk outside the Tiangong space station, matching Chen Dong's record. The seven-hour excursion was completed alongside crewmate Wu Fei.

title: Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class
summary: NASA's newest astronaut candidate class, named 'Platypi,' is training at Johnson Space Center to prepare for future missions to the ISS, Moon, and Mars. The group of ten explorers was selected in 2025.

title: NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has successfully completed final prelaunch tests, including extreme sound exposure, shaking, and electronic checks, confirming its readiness for launch conditions and space operations.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Australia's Central Australia experienced significant rainfall in early 2026, turning the desert landscape green. This unusual weather event transformed the region's arid environment.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying the contacts between the boxwork unit and the layered sulfate unit. The mission is led by Catherine O'Connell-Cooper from the University of New Brunswick, Canada.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: The Operational Land Imager (OLI) on Landsat 8 captured a rare clear image of Mayon, the Philippines' most active volcano, on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows with infrared data. The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS) reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds on that day.
company: Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS)

title: From Service to Space Systems: A Pathways Journey to NASA
summary: Corey Elmore transitioned from military service to a NASA Pathways engineering internship at Kennedy Space Center, gaining hands-on experience in technical processes and tools through the Pathways program.

title: Widely Attended Gatherings (WAGs) Determinations
summary: The news lists various upcoming space industry events and gatherings scheduled for 2026, including the Maryland Space Business Roundtable, SIA Annual Leadership Dinner, and Artemis Suppliers Conference.

title: NASA’s Hubble Unexpectedly Catches Comet Breaking Up
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured the rare event of comet K1 (C/2025 K1 ATLAS) breaking apart. The findings were published in the journal Icarus.

title: Wave of Dust Rolls Through Texas
summary: A cold front caused a significant dust storm across the Texas high plains, creating a dense wall of sand and small particles.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft is preparing for its second flight, marking a step toward further testing in 2026. The upcoming flights will focus on envelope expansion to validate safety and performance.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another step forward for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: This week features nine planned launches, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. The launches mark a busy period for the space industry.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept highlights Blue Origin's expanding role in space safety and exploration.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for lunar and Martian missions, highlighted by the transformation of its Starfactory facility to produce Starship vehicles. The company is repurposing infrastructure originally designed for steel production to support its space ambitions.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) saw new crew arrivals and highlighted scientific activities in February following the early departure of Crew-11 in mid-January due to a medical concern.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 dominating the launch schedule. The post highlights the busy week ahead in space missions.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has entered a new testing phase with Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a significant milestone since Flight 11 in October 2025. This development highlights SpaceX's ongoing advancements in reusable rocket technology.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Alpha FLTA007 rocket, marking its return to flight after a nearly 10-month pause. The mission, dubbed 'Stairway to Seven,' signifies a major milestone for the company's launch capabilities.

title: Baikonur Launch Pad restored, clearing path for Progress MS-33 ISS Mission
summary: Russia's Baikonur Cosmodrome's Site 31/6 launch pad has been fully restored, enabling the upcoming Progress MS-33 mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This repair marks a critical step in maintaining Russia's ISS resupply capabilities.

title: NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced significant changes to the Artemis program to restore its momentum, though key details are still being finalized.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on the space industry without specifying a particular news item.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch in January 2027 aboard an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its suppliers to use the new costing software for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, likely used for simulating microgravity or spacecraft dynamics. This initiative aims to provide hands-on experience in space-related research.

title: Annular solar eclipse seen from space
summary: A rare annular solar eclipse was captured from space by ESA's Proba-2 satellite, providing a unique view of the celestial event from Earth's orbit.

title: ESA’s 5G laboratory on wheels
summary: ESA has developed a mobile 5G laboratory to test and demonstrate next-generation connectivity solutions. This innovative setup allows for flexible and on-site testing of 5G technologies in various environments.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital flight of the SR75 rocket, scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demo mission planned for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket has undergone modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster margins, with its launch planned for this summer. These changes were in development before a recent anomaly, and the company is accelerating their implementation.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. This investment highlights the growing interest in alternative navigation technologies.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated regulations. This marks the end of legacy regulations for the industry.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet rising demand, but policymakers can accelerate growth according to two industry groups.

title: Nvidia Unveils New Hardware to Unlock Orbital Data Centers
summary: Nvidia has introduced new hardware designed to enable the development of orbital data centers, which could revolutionize data processing in space. The announcement provides insights into the future architecture of space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Canadian Launchers and Spaceport Get Funding Boost
summary: Canada will invest in a spaceport and three young rocket systems to enhance its sovereign launch capabilities. This funding boost aims to strengthen the country's space industry.

title: Leonardo’s Missile-Defense Demo in Ukraine Aims To Be a Blueprint for Other Nations
summary: Leonardo is conducting a missile-defense demonstration in Ukraine, aiming to establish a model for other nations. The company seeks to become a major distributor of space-enabled missile defense systems.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a defense with the FCC stating that its SkyTerra Next proposal will not cause interference. The proposal involves hosting an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites within Ligado's existing L-band coordination.

title: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics to Build Solar Arrays for K-LEO
summary: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics will jointly build solar arrays for K-LEO, South Korea's national security constellation. The project is part of South Korea's sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite initiative.

title: Connectivity Under Fire: The Critical Need for Satcom Terminal Survivability
summary: The article discusses the importance of satcom terminal survivability in contested environments for U.S. military platforms, highlighting the need for beyond-line-of-sight connectivity for command and control and mission execution.

title: Satellogic Plans Next-Gen Merlin EO Constellation
summary: Satellogic announced plans for its next-generation 'Merlin' Earth observation satellites, which will offer one-meter resolution and daily mapping capabilities. The company aims to enhance its Earth observation services with this new constellation.

title: 9 New Faces for SATShow Week 2026
summary: SATShow Week 2026 will introduce nine new speakers, expanding its focus on military space with GovMilSpace in its second year. The event aims to bring together key industry leaders in the satellite sector.

title: Industry Roundtable With ’10 Hottest’ Executives
summary: Via Satellite interviewed executives from the '10 Hottest Companies in Satellite' for 2026 ahead of SATShow Week to discuss industry trends and insights.

title: Driving Ubiquitous Connectivity: Multi-Orbit Satellite Architecture and Advanced User Terminals
summary: The article discusses advancements in multi-orbit satellite architecture and advanced user terminals to enhance ubiquitous connectivity. It highlights innovations in satellite technology to improve global communication coverage.

title: Telesat’s Lightspeed Service Launch Slips to 2028
summary: Telesat's Lightspeed LEO constellation launch has been delayed to 2028 due to ASIC delays for satellite processors. The service was originally planned to start earlier.

title: Nvidia Launches New Chips for Space Missions
summary: Nvidia announced new chips designed for space missions, targeting applications such as orbital data centers, geospatial intelligence, and autonomous space operations. The move involves collaboration with space companies like Aetherflux.

title: Canada to Invest in New Spaceport in Nova Scotia
summary: Canada is investing in a new spaceport in Nova Scotia to enhance its space capabilities. The Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency (ACOA) is allocating $200 million for this project.
